'''Jabrogg Thung''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] who lived during the last decades of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. He partook in the sport of [[nerf-throwing]], and became the intergalactic nerf-throwing champion. During his sporting career, he [[Death/Legends|fatally]] injured several [[Nerf/Legends|nerfs]], which lead to the [[Galactic Humane Society]] placing a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on his head. The [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] encountered Thung on the [[Outland Transit Station]] in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]] and captured him alive, claiming the bounty for 2,000 credits.

==Biography==
By the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], Thung had taken up a career in the sport known as [[nerf-throwing]] and, while working as an [[athlete]], he was responsible for the [[Death/Legends|fatal]] injury of several [[Nerf/Legends|nerfs]], a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|non-sentient]] grazing animal. Thung's prowess in the sport led to him being considered the intergalactic nerf-throwing champion, but his involvement in the death of the nerfs resulted in the [[Galactic Humane Society]] placing a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on his head. The organization offered 2,000 credits for his capture alive, but would only pay 1,000 credits if he was killed. By 32 BBY, Thugg's career had faltered and he scratched out a living as a hired thug on the crime-infested [[Space station/Legends|space station]] [[Outland Transit Station|Outland]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. While Thung was there, the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] also arrived on the station and begun hunting down the [[Muttani]] crime boss [[Meeko Ghintee]] as a favor to the station's [[Toydarian/Legends|Toydarian]] owner, [[Rozatta]].<ref name="BH" />

{{Gamemechanics}}
While in pursuit of a fleeing Ghintee, Thung, accompanied by several other Gamorreans and [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshans]], attacked the Mandalorian. However, Fett managed to successfully capture the former sports champion alive and later claimed the bounty of 2,000 from the Society.<ref name="BH" />
{{Endgame}}

==Personality and traits==
Jabrogg Thung was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] Gamorrean with [[Color/Legends|green]] skin and yellow [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. Like most Gamorreans, he possessed exceptional strength and resilience. A hardened criminal, he did not hesitate to try and kill Fett when the two crossed paths.<ref name="BH" />

==Equipment==
In combat, Thung used a [[Vibro-ax/Legends|Vibro-ax]] and wore brown [[Armor/Legends|armor]], including a helmet.<ref name="BH" />

==Behind the scenes==
Thung first appeared in the video game ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'', developed by [[LucasArts]] and released in [[2002]]. In the game, Jabrogg is the second of five possible secondary bounties that a player can secure in the pit fighters' training yard in Chapter One. Players have the option of taking him alive or killing him, but this article assumes that he was captured alive as this is the option with the highest reward. He also was mentioned in the [[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Bounty Hunter'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]], which was released along side the game by [[Prima]].
